title: Joins, Cardinality, Unmatched Rows, and Fanout Control · Query Retrieval
summary: Ticket, team, owner, and event data must be combined without losing unassigned work or multiplying effort totals. Learners retrieve rules and procedures before seeing feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a cardinality-safe support investigation after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Write explicit inner joins from relationship predicates and predict result cardinality before execution.
Use left, right, or full outer-join reasoning to retain required unmatched entities.
Model and query many-to-many relationships through an associative relation with its own grain.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

SELECT sqlite_version() AS browser_sqlite_version;

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Fanout Diagnosis
steps.instruction:
review case — Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 4 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure.

Inspect the Fanout Diagnosis trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Separate evidence from confidence · Review Fanout Diagnosis.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for detect join fanout with pre/post counts and repair aggregation at the correct grain. For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Fanout Diagnosis, keep Fanout Diagnosis connected to executable relational evidence.

content.text: For Fanout Diagnosis, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Fanout Diagnosis, name the row grain, Fanout Diagnosis precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Separate evidence from confidence · Review Fanout Diagnosis, trace this concrete input and predict rows or changes before running it: Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 4 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure.

For Separate evidence from confidence · Review Fanout Diagnosis, challenge the documented misconception with result, constraint, or plan evidence: DISTINCT is a safe universal repair for duplicated join results.

stimulus.title: Fanout Diagnosis case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: Report every team with ticket count, unresolved count, and total event minutes. Team Drift has no tickets and ticket 101 has 4 events; predict result grain and prove joins do not multiply either measure.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: DISTINCT is a safe universal repair for duplicated join results.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Detect join fanout with pre/post counts and repair aggregation at the correct grain. Artifact under review: a cardinality-safe support investigation.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Fanout Diagnosis, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.
Run a convenient Fanout Diagnosis query for separate evidence from confidence, omit grain, NULL, cardinality, and dialect checks, then accept one plausible result grid.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Detect join fanout with pre/post counts and repair aggregation at the correct grain.
